mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without regime dependence.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of t-1mo suggests room for easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a 2-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and triggering equity risk-off positioning. This threshold crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of t-1mo, currently below trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 2-month period indicates weakening aggregate demand. This interaction signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns as forward earnings expectations decline across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% and HOUST declines > 5% within 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 723853M and HOUST at 1366K as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails to outperform single-series signals (Rank IC difference < 0.01), or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines below 102.5 over a 3-month rolling average, signaling supply-side weakness and stagflationary pressure. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as real earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.511 as of t-2mo, currently above trigger
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO weakness are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market momentum loss. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of months where PAYEMS growth is below 100K, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 320 index points while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.5%, creating a real rate compression regime. This interaction signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as negative real rates support equity valuations relative to bonds.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 319.775 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate over a 2-quarter window, signaling economic slowdown. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in late-cycle expansions but neutral returns in early-cycle recoveries.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5% over 2-quarter window
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% in late-cycle regimes, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1400K units over a 2-month rolling average, signaling robust residential investment and forward demand strength. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as construction activity signals economic confidence.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1366K as of t-2mo, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where HOUST exceeds 1400K, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
